subject: Ecology
title: Soil Sealing
description: A quick, practical guide to Soil Sealing.

hook.text: Every new building, road, and parking lot permanently seals living soil under concrete.

buildup.text: Sealed soil can't absorb water, cycle nutrients, support life, or store carbon.

discovery.text: Europe loses over 1,000 square kilometers of soil to sealing every year.

twist.text: Once sealed, restoring soil function is nearly impossible—the ecosystem beneath is entombed.

climax.text: Policies that mandate permeable surfaces can slow sealing and protect soil for future use.

quiz.question: What happens to soil sealed under concrete?
options: It loses all ecological function permanently
It becomes better at filtering water
